name: Baked Crab, Pepper and Spinach Frittata
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Fresh Spinach (1 Large Bunch) - wash, stems removed
2/3 cup Asiago or Parmesan Cheese - coarsely grated
8 oz Dungeness Crab Meat - well picked over
recipeInstructions:
1. Saute the onion and garlic in olive oil over moderate heat until soft but not brown.
2. Blanch the spinach in lightly salted boiling water for a few seconds until wilted. Drain and immediately plunge into ice water to stop cooking and set color. Drain again and gently squeeze out as much moisture as possible with a clean tea towel. Coarsely chop the spinach and combine with onion mixture. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
3. Char the peppers over an open flame or under a broiler until all sides are blackened. Remove and cover loosely with plastic for a few minutes to sweat. Then scrape off charred skin with the point of a knife. Remove seeds and stems, and cut into long, thick slices.
4. Lightly butter or oil a small terrine or loaf pan (8x4x2 1/2 inches) and line the bottom with buttered wax paper or parchment.
5. Beat the eggs briefly together with herbs and season with salt and pepper. Layer one third of spinach mixture on the bottom of the pan, followed by one third of roasted red peppers, one third of cheese, and one third of crab. Pour about one third of egg mixture over the top and gently tap the pan to distribute evenly.
6. Continue layering in this order until all ingredients are used.
7. Place terrine in a larger baking pan and pour enough hot tap water to come at least two-thirds up the sides of the terrine. Preheat oven to 325°F, cover with parchment or foil, and bake until eggs are just set. Remove from water bath and let sit 15-20 minutes before unmolding if serving warm.
8. Yield: 6 servings
